title: eriogonum umbellatum subsp. minus
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. minus, also known as dwarf sulphur flower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buckwheat family. It is native to the western United States and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and relieving respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. minus can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies.
